_note: Per-ENGINE prompt-adaptation rules the generator auto-injects so every prompt removes that engine's known weaknesses. Data (craft), edited freely; loaded by generate-shot-pack.cjs --engine=<key>. Distilled from the Hell Grind / commercial workflows + reference clips. Verify caps as engines update.

seedance.label: Seedance 2.0 (CS 2.0) — text/image-to-video
rules: HARD CAP: keep each shot prompt <= 3000 characters. If longer, tighten — one main idea + one main action + one camera strategy per shot.

Seedance historically performs better with CHINESE prompts AND Chinese packs ~1-2 chars/word (vs 5-10 English), fitting more info under the 3000-char cap. Offer a Chinese variant for dense shots (verify current behavior).

SPATIAL AWARENESS is the weakness: NEVER stage a character ENTERING from outside INTO a room (it invents extra doors / teleports). Put the CAMERA INSIDE facing inward; push the camera FURTHER IN to kill the 'too many doors' artifact.
ONE audio track only: environmental SFX only, NO music, NO subtitles (music can't be separated in post — add music when editing).
REFERENCE ORDER matters: the asset described first in the prompt must be attached first (hero before location).
Spell out CAMERA MOVE + SHOT SIZE per beat (push-in medium->closeup, super-low-angle on boots). Beats that don't land -> make the shot longer + complete the action.
Put FAILURE-MODE HANDLES inside the prompt ('do not drift the <small prop>'), not as side-notes.
Batch 8, skim for composition, stop + fix the prompt if several share the same mistake (~1/64 usable).

nano_banana_pro.label: Nano Banana Pro / GPT-image — still image generation
rules: FIGHT PLASTICKY (its default): add 'light atmospheric haze' + 'film grain' + 'crush the blacks for shadow depth' + specific practical-light keywords.
Weak SPATIAL AWARENESS: describe a NEW location in WORDS, do NOT upload a location image into the generator (it crams uploaded rooms in — e.g. a room outdoors).
Batch 4: with a detailed prompt it's consistent, so if something's off the PROMPT is the bug — fix it, don't just re-roll.
Editing an image loses detail each pass: re-edit from the clean SOURCE, or regenerate FROM SCRATCH with the full prompt + no reference to lock a clean version.
Generate locations at 3/4 or CCTV angle (never head-on) for depth the video model can hold.
Character/product SHEETS: gray background (#7A7A7A), single-face (erase duplicates), closeup + full-body panels — higher win-rate.

kling.label: Kling — image/text-to-video
rules: Strong at smooth realistic human motion — describe motion move-by-move (not 'he dances': 'two head nods, shoulder roll, knee dip on the beat').
Lock start (+ optionally end) frame for a controlled hero motion; describe only the motion between.
Keep the style-prefix look consistent; env-SFX audio discipline as with any single-track engine.

higgsfield.label: Higgsfield — generate_image / generate_video (multi-model)
rules: Use generate_image for the start/end FRAMES and character/prop sheets, generate_video for the motion between.
models_explore(action:'recommend') to pick the best model per shot before generating; upscale/reframe/motion_control for edits (don't re-generate).
virality_predictor on the cut for a hook/retention read; keep the env-SFX audio discipline.
